lines.line: # Family Background

> Greg Davis discusses his family history, including his position in the birth order and growing up in Hartford. He shares about being raised in a single-parent home by his mother who is about to celebrate her 93rd birthday.
** family history, Hartford, single-parent home, mother, birthday **

lines.line: # Education and Aspirations
> Greg talks about his mother's aspirations for him and his brothers to attend college. He describes his educational journey, initially wanting to be a teacher, and later deciding on law school.
** education, college, law school, aspirations, teaching **

story.title: Greg Davis Oral History
story.description: The interview with Greg Davis centers around his personal and familial background, career path, community involvement, and perspectives on living in Bloomfield. Greg shares insights into his upbringing in a single-parent household, educational journey from Loomis to Tufts, and his career in law. A significant part of his narrative emphasizes his involvement with the Boys and Girls Club and other community services, illustrating the role of mentorship and service in shaping his identity. Greg discusses Bloomfield as a diverse and evolving town, expressing optimism about its growth but highlighting a need for economic development alongside preserving its rich heritage. Throughout, he underscores themes of community, belonging, and the interplay between personal heritage and civic engagement.
